title: 10 Irrational Best-Picture Oscar Predictions and Why ‘Belfast’ Is the Front-Runner
format: The show goes back to its Oscar roots, 131 days out from the ceremony. Belfast as the early front-runner, a run through the ten likely Best Picture nominees, a Will Smith stock check, and Sean's interview with Alison Klayman about her Alanis Morissette documentary, Jagged.

films.title: Belfast
films.note: Kenneth Branagh's autobiographical film, told from the perspective of nine-year-old Buddy as the Troubles break out in 1969 Northern Ireland. Sean saw it at Telluride; a sweet, sincere crowd-pleaser scored largely by Van Morrison. Amanda loved it (and the two very beautiful parents) until she remembered it might win Best Picture. Jude Hill, Caitríona Balfe, Jamie Dornan, Judi Dench, and Ciarán Hinds all in the awards mix.

films.title: Roma
films.note: The inevitable comparison: Alfonso Cuarón's black-and-white childhood remembrance. Sean calls it stylistically and intellectually more dynamic than Belfast.

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: Amanda's other touchstone: political turmoil swirling around a small kid, hitting the same crowd-pleasing sweet spot, minus the Hitler jokes.

films.title: Moonlight
films.note: Cited as the kind of small, deeply personal film the Academy has rewarded against a franchise-dominated industry.

films.title: Green Book
films.note: The other 'for better or worse' precedent: an intimate, small-cast winner Belfast has something in common with.

films.title: Henry V
films.note: The bravura breakthrough; the hosts agree his Shakespeare adaptations are his best films.

films.title: Much Ado About Nothing
films.note: Amanda's favorite of the big-budget Shakespeare, ridiculous but fun, from the Emma Thompson era.

films.title: Hamlet
films.note: Part of the case for Branagh as the modern Olivier and Shakespeare's ambassador to pop culture.

films.title: Dead Again
films.note: Sean had just watched it, an Emma Thompson neo-noir/screwball that has no idea what it wants to be.

films.title: Mary Shelley's Frankenstein
films.note: The famous overwrought bomb that followed.

films.title: Thor
films.note: Exhibit A for the decade of commercial work-for-hire that left him 'in the wilderness.'

films.title: Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit
films.note: Kevin Costner and Chris Pine rebooting Jack Ryan; should have worked, didn't.

films.title: Cinderella
films.note: The Lily James Disney adaptation, a big hit the hosts thought was fine.

films.title: Murder on the Orient Express
films.note: Amanda's beef: he 'ruined the canon.' A solid hit all the same.

films.title: Artemis Fowl
films.note: One of the most reviled films of 2020, a Disney+ casualty of the pandemic.

films.title: Sense and Sensibility
films.note: Invoked via Greg Wise (Mr. Willoughby), whom Emma Thompson later married. Part of Amanda forgiving Branagh.

films.title: The Power of the Dog
films.note: Jane Campion's return to form, widely hailed; the other presumed front-runner. Neither host has seen it yet; it opens December 1. Sean's nagging hunch: a late Benedict Cumberbatch surprise.

films.title: King Richard
films.note: Opening at week's end; treated as a Best Picture lock on the strength of Will Smith.

films.title: Dune
films.note: The only wide-release blockbuster with real clout in the race, the 'Mad Max spot.' Sean doesn't think it's a lock; Amanda talks herself into it.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Unseen but penciled in on Spielberg and Kushner alone. Amanda is scarred by Ariana DeBose's Oscar pre-show and pinning her hopes on Rachel Zegler.

films.title: The Lost Daughter
films.note: Maggie Gyllenhaal's Elena Ferrante adaptation, which Sean thought was fantastic; Olivia Colman all but guarantees it a slot.

films.title: The Tragedy of Macbeth
films.note: Joel Coen's black-and-white Macbeth with Frances McDormand and Denzel Washington; very warm reviews from the New York Film Festival, A24 and Apple.

films.title: Being the Ricardos
films.note: Aaron Sorkin on Lucille Ball and Desi Arnaz; a strong first screening despite doubts and rough trailers.

films.title: Nightmare Alley
films.note: Guillermo del Toro, Academy-beloved; unseen but slotted in as one of the two most likely remaining nominees.

films.title: CODA
films.note: Apple's Sundance buy; not the phenomenon Ted Lasso was, but 'if people actually watch it, they'll enjoy it.' Amanda's tenth pick as a test of Apple.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: Adam McKay's DiCaprio/Lawrence satire; star-studded but a comedy, which historically doesn't play: 'if it's more comedy than commentary, it might be on the outside looking in.'

films.title: House of Gucci
films.note: Mixed reviews, but the hosts loved it. Amanda's rant: bully an Academy voter in your life into embracing it, and put Jared Leto back at the center of the race.

films.title: Licorice Pizza
films.note: Paul Thomas Anderson's new film, another Phantom Thread situation where it's never obvious when the Academy logs on with PTA.

films.title: Spencer
films.note: Discussed the prior week; perhaps too weird for Academy tastes ('or boring, if you're Amanda').

films.title: Passing
films.note: Rebecca Hall's directorial debut, just on Netflix. Sean rates the performances, Tessa Thompson and especially Ruth Negga, over the film; a possible below-the-line play in costume or production design.

films.title: Tick, Tick... Boom!
films.note: Lin-Manuel Miranda's Jonathan Larson quasi-musical with Andrew Garfield; Sean liked it a lot. Second-tier, likely just outside.

films.title: Parallel Mothers
films.note: Pedro Almodóvar's New York Film Festival entry, out at year's end.

films.title: Swan Song
films.note: Benjamin Cleary's soft-sci-fi Apple film: Mahershala Ali's first solo lead, a standout performance.

films.title: The Tender Bar
films.note: George Clooney's J.R. Moehringer adaptation with Ben Affleck; delightful, but not a Best Picture contender.

films.title: Cyrano
films.note: Joe Wright's musical with Peter Dinklage.

films.title: A Journal for Jordan
films.note: Denzel Washington directing, Michael B. Jordan starring; Denzel's own voiceover closes the trailer.

films.title: The Matrix Resurrections
films.note: Unseen; Sean doubts it's an Oscar movie but loves the trailer.

films.title: A Hero
films.note: Asghar Farhadi's latest morality play, 'the most consistent filmmaker in the world.' A Best International Feature contender Sean thinks belongs in the Best Picture race.

films.title: The Last Duel
films.note: A box-office bomb Sean rates among the year's best; a marketing failure for leaving Ben Affleck out of the trailer.

films.title: Red Rocket
films.note: Sean Baker's film with Simon Rex; Sean has a lot of feelings, saved for next week.

films.title: C'mon C'mon
films.note: Mike Mills's new film: wonderful performances, makes them feel good about the world, but doesn't read as an Oscar contender.

films.title: The French Dispatch
films.note: Wes Anderson's ensemble, considered slight by some and doing soft business, but as good to Sean's taste as most of the field.

segments.heading: Alison Klayman on Jagged
segments.note: the Music Box entry on Alanis Morissette

films.title: Jagged
films.note: Klayman's intimate portrait of Alanis Morissette around Jagged Little Pill, part of Ringer Films and HBO's Music Box series (Sean is a producer). Built around a two-day master interview and hundreds of hours of Morissette's own storage-unit archive, shot as one of the first COVID-era productions. Morissette is not promoting the film.

films.title: Ai Weiwei: Never Sorry
films.note: Klayman's earlier subject-driven portrait, invoked for how she embeds with her subjects.

films.title: The Brink
films.note: Her Steve Bannon film: the other case where the main character didn't tour the movie with her.

films.title: The Velvet Underground
films.note: Todd Haynes's doc, cited for the 'only people who were there' approach Klayman shares.

films.title: Don't Go Telling Your Mama
films.note: Klayman's last-great-thing: a ~40-minute visual album by Topaz Jones and Rubberband, inspired by the Black ABCs, which she was judging for DOC NYC.

TV: The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon

The French Dispatch.title: The French Dispatch of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun
